commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3ad48ca00c230d829149e77c682a07503aa56ca6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes gateway hook target resolution and tests so explicit unknown `/hooks/agent` targets return 400 instead of routing to the default agent, and adds a CHANGELOG entry.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests 0, Docs +1. Total +5 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main falls back unknown hook `agentId` values to the default agent and the existing gateway tests encode that behavior. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review should decide whether removing the documented fallback is acceptable; after that decision, the docs, error text, and changelog fixes are mechanical.

target.title: Reject unknown hook agent targets
target.body:
## Summary
- Reject explicit `/hooks/agent` requests that name an unknown `agentId` instead of silently falling back to the default agent
- Preserve default-agent routing for omitted/blank `agentId` when the default target is allowlisted
- Update gateway hook helper/integration tests and changelog coverage

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Explicit `/hooks/agent` calls with an unknown `agentId` must be rejected instead of silently falling back to the default agent when hook routing is allowlisted.

**Real environment tested:** Local branch-built OpenClaw Gateway from PR commit `d41018a879`, isolated temporary state directory, Gateway bound to `127.0.0.1:19089`, config with default agent `veritas`, `hooks.enabled=true`, and `hooks.allowedAgentIds=["veritas"]`. Channels/providers were skipped for the proof instance so only the Gateway hook route behavior was exercised.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Started the branch-built Gateway with the isolated config, then ran these live `curl` requests against `/hooks/agent`:

```console
$ curl -s -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19089/hooks/agent \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message":"proof","agentId":"not-a-real-agent","deliver":false}'

$ curl -s -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19089/hooks/agent \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message":"proof","deliver":false,"timeoutSeconds":1}'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output from the branch-built Gateway:

```console
Live proof: /hooks/agent unknown agentId rejection
commit: d41018a879
config: hooks.allowedAgentIds=[veritas], default agent=veritas

Unknown explicit agentId request
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

{"ok":false,"error":"agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds"}

Omitted agentId control request
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

{"ok":true,"runId":"810d7b9e-ef28-4281-bdfe-2db69568fc39"}
```

**Observed result:** The live unknown-agent curl returned HTTP 400 and the JSON error `agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds`. The live omitted-agent control curl returned HTTP 200 with run id `810d7b9e-ef28-4281-bdfe-2db69568fc39`, confirming default-agent routing still works for allowed default targets.

**Not tested:** No additional gaps. The proof used a minimal Gateway with channels/providers skipped because this change is limited to Gateway hook target validation before provider execution.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/hooks.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts --quiet`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md src/gateway/hooks.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed --changed origin/main`
target.author: bradgroux
